div.p: TTL (Time-To-Live) decrements at each hop. When TTL=0, message stops propagating.

g.text: Action: Multicast to B, C, D

div.p: Required TTL for full coverage: TTL ≥ ⌈log_fanout(N)⌉ For N=8 nodes, fanout=3: log₃(8) ≈ 1.89 → TTL ≥ 2

p.strong: Why TTL=3 fails for Node H:

ul.li: Hop 3: E,F,G→H (TTL: 1→0, expires) Message reaches H but TTL=0 prevents H from processing.

div.p: ⚠️ TTL Too Low = Incomplete Coverage With TTL=3 in 8-node cluster:

ul.li: Node H receives expired data Rule: Set TTL ≥ log_fanout(N) × 1.5

div.p: 💡 TTL + Seen Cache Interaction TTL is a safety net. The seen-message cache (LRU) stops duplicates before TTL exhaustion in healthy clusters.
